title: ROUGE: Recall-Focused Evaluation
concept: ROUGE (Recall-Oriented Understudy for Gisting Evaluation) measures how much of the human reference summary was captured by the model.

exercises.prompt: For which of the following tasks is ROUGE most commonly used?
exercises.explain: ROUGE is the standard metric used to evaluate how well an AI can summarize a longer text by comparing its output to human references.

cards.title: Did the AI Miss Anything?
cards.body: When an AI summarizes a long article, how do we know if it missed the most important points? We need a way to measure how much of the key information actually made it into the summary. That is where ROUGE comes to save the day!

cards.title: What is ROUGE?
cards.body: ROUGE stands for Recall-Oriented Understudy for Gisting Evaluation. It is a metric that evaluates AI-generated summaries by comparing them to human-written reference summaries. It asks: "How much of the essential information did the AI manage to keep?"

cards.title: The Grocery Checklist
cards.body: Imagine you are going grocery shopping with a checklist written by your roommate. If the checklist has 10 items, and you successfully bring home 8 of them, your 'recall' is 80%! ROUGE is just like that checklist test—it checks how many of the required 'ideas' your AI successfully brought home.

cards.title: ROUGE in Action
cards.body:
Reference (Human): "The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog." (9 words)
AI Summary: "The quick brown fox jumps." (5 matching words)

Here, the AI captured 5 out of the 9 reference words. ROUGE measures this overlap to give a score showing how much of the original meaning was recalled.

cards.title: Perfect Score != Perfect Writing
cards.body:
Myth: A high ROUGE score means the summary is beautifully written and grammatically perfect.
Fact: ROUGE only looks at overlapping words and phrases. An AI summary could have a high ROUGE score but still sound a bit clunky or repetitive to a human reader.

cards.title: Comparing Reference vs. Candidate
cards.body: See how ROUGE compares what the AI generated against the gold standard human reference.

left.text: "The cat slept on the warm mat." (Total information needed)

exercises.prompt: What does the acronym ROUGE stand for?

options.text: Random Output Generator for Easy Evaluation
Rapid Output Utility for General English
Readability Option for Universal Grammar Evaluation

exercises.explain: ROUGE stands for Recall-Oriented Understudy for Gisting Evaluation. It focuses heavily on recall to ensure key details aren't left out.

exercises.prompt: True or False: ROUGE is primarily used to evaluate text summarization tasks.
exercises.explain: ROUGE specifically measures the overlap between the candidate text and the reference text to see how much information was successfully recalled.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to complete the description of ROUGE evaluation.
exercises.sentence: ROUGE measures how much of the reference summary written by a ___ is captured by the AI.
exercises.explain: ROUGE evaluates AI summaries by comparing them directly against gold-standard reference summaries written by human experts.

exercises.prompt: Given the human reference: "The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog." Which of the following summaries is likely a poor AI generation?

options.text: Summary A: "A quick brown fox jumped."
Summary B: "The lazy dog ate the fox."
Both are equal quality AI summaries.

exercises.explain: Summary B is the AI-generated text because it contains incorrect information ("ate the fox") which contradicts the human reference, while Summary A is a accurate condensed version.

exercises.prompt: Why is ROUGE considered a recall-focused metric for text summarization?

options.text: It calculates Precision by focusing only on the AI's length.
It calculates Recall by checking how much of the human summary was captured.
It ignores the human summary completely.
It only counts the total number of characters.

exercises.explain: ROUGE is highly recall-focused because it divides the matching words by the total words in the reference (human) summary, ensuring the AI didn't leave out important details.
